channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: A reação de Valdemar à pré-candidatura de Ronaldo Caiado ao Planalto
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL party, expressed doubts about Ronaldo Caiado's pre-candidacy for president under the PSD, stating that the 2026 election runoff will be between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. He suggested Caiado, despite being right-wing, will ultimately support Bolsonaro's son, and urged all right-wing candidates to unite behind Flávio in the first round to secure victory over Lula.

item.title: PL entra com pedido no STF para reivindicar que futuro presidente da Alerj assuma como governador interino
item.description: The PL filed an amicus curiae brief with the STF to ensure that the future president of Alerj, Douglas Ruas, assumes as interim governor, upholding the constitutional succession line over the interim appointment of Judge Ricardo Couto ordered by Minister Cristiano Zanin. The party argues that Article 141 of the state constitution establishes succession through the Legislative Assembly, and that the debate over the direct election format for the interim mandate should not override the interim rule. The STF will review the PSD’s direct election action on April 8.

item.title: Deputados propõem lei que pode classificar como antissemita quem faz críticas a Israel
item.description: A bill proposed in Brazil's Chamber of Deputies seeks to define antisemitism based on the IHRA working definition, potentially allowing criticism of Israel to be classified as antisemitic depending on interpretation. Supported by lawmakers across the political spectrum, including Tabata Amaral and Kim Kataguiri, the bill does not create new crimes but links the issue to the Racism Law, which could affect how existing legislation is applied. Authors state the goal is to clarify public policy, not to restrict political debate within constitutional limits.

item.title: Sucessão no Rio: PL tenta derrubar decisão que preteriu presidente da Alerj do governo interino
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) has asked the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to overturn Minister Cristiano Zanin's decision maintaining Rio de Janeiro State Justice Tribunal President Ricardo Couto as interim governor. The PL contends that the state constitution mandates that the future president of the Legislative Assembly (Alerj) automatically assume the governorship, and seeks to install deputy Douglas Ruas in that role to serve as interim governor until the extraordinary election. The party argues the STF ruling violates constitutional succession rules by favoring a judicial official lacking popular legitimacy over an elected legislator.

item.title: Kassab explica escolha de Caiado
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, announced that Ronaldo Caiado was selected as the party's presidential candidate for 2026 due to his higher electoral chances. The decision followed an internal contest with Rio Grande do Sul governor Eduardo Leite. Kassab stressed the choice was based on electoral viability assessments.

item.title: Moraes dá 24h para Bolsonaro explicar fala de Eduardo sobre gravação de vídeo para o pai
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes gave Bolsonaro's defense 24 hours to explain a statement by deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro regarding a video recorded for his father. The order is part of investigations into potential abuse of power and misinformation. Eduardo's comment suggested the video could serve as evidence if something happened to the ex-president.

item.title: Michelle nega ter recebido vídeo de Eduardo
item.description: First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro denied receiving a video from former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro, who claimed he had recorded the material to show to his father, Jair Bolsonaro, during CPAC in the U.S. Eduardo's statement prompted Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to order Bolsonaro's defense to explain a possible violation of house arrest measures. Moraes considers the remarks may constitute a breach of the imposed restrictions.

item.title: Análise: Ao se equilibrar entre os 'valores do Brasil profundo' e o gestor moderno, Caiado tem tarefa quixotesca em eleição polarizada por Lula e Flávio
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD-GO), aged 76, faces a highly polarized presidential election between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, attempting to balance his image as a modern manager with appeal to the 'values of Brazil's heartland'. His profile as an 'obstinate aspirant of the second way of the right' highlights the challenges of positioning himself in a landscape dominated by two antagonistic figures, where his candidacy is seen as quixotic due to the difficulty of gaining traction in a deeply divided electorate.

item.title: Lula manda estudar novos limites para juros do rotativo
item.description: President Lula has directed the economic team to study new limits on credit card revolving interest rates, which currently reach 435.9% per year, comparing the modality to the overdraft facility, which already has a fixed rate of 8%. Although a rule exists capping interest at 100% of the debt (limiting total to R$2,000 for a R$1,000 debt), it has not been effectively implemented, allowing revolving credit to remain the most expensive credit line in the country. The goal is to reduce household indebtedness, given that 78.9% of families were in debt by the end of 2025 and 73.3 million Brazilians were delinquent in January 2026.

item.title: Geração autocracia cresceu com esquerda no poder e busca direita radical como solução
item.description: Brazilian youth aged 16 to 24 show growing support for the radical right, led by Renan Santos of the Missão party and Flávio Bolsonaro, with voting intentions reaching up to 37% in this age group. This movement, fueled by anti-state and religious rhetoric, seeks gradual autocratization through the erosion of fundamental rights, despite formally defending electoral democracy. The AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll reveals a pronounced gender gap, with men significantly more likely to support these candidacies.

item.title: A preferência de Valdemar para a vice de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, said he is 'rooting' for Flávio Bolsonaro to choose a woman as his running mate in 2026, claiming women are 'much better' than men in every way. He also criticized the 2022 choice of Walter Braga Netto as vice president, stating the general brought no votes to Bolsonaro and suggested Tereza Cristina would have been a better pick.

item.title: Gleisi diz que Lula pediu estudos sobre rotativo do cartão de crédito em meio a preocupação com endividamento das famílias
item.description: Minister Gleisi Hoffmann revealed that President Lula requested studies from the Central Bank and the Ministry of Finance to lower credit card revolving interest rates, which he described as unjustifiably high. Lula linked household debt from online purchases of cheap goods to public frustration blamed on the government, while affirming that debt for asset-building is acceptable.

item.title: Governo Tarcísio inicia audiências sobre concessão de Complexo do Ibirapuera e Vila Olímpica
item.description: The government of Tarcísio de Freitas held public hearings in April to present a proposal for the concession of the Ibirapuera Sports Complex and Vila Olímpica Mário Covas to private entities, aiming to improve infrastructure and expand public access to sports and leisure activities. The project, previously attempted by governors João Doria (2019) and Geraldo Alckmin (2017), faces opposition due to the site’s heritage status granted by Iphan in 2024, following an earlier proposal that included demolition and commercial development such as shopping centers and modern arenas.

item.title: Gleisi diz que PSD preferiu “figura mais agressiva” com Caiado
item.description: Gleisi Hoffmann stated that the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its 2026 presidential candidate increases electoral polarization, as he is seen as a more aggressive figure than Eduardo Leite. She argued this leaves little room for a competitive third way and commented on the political situation in Paraná, where she will run for Senate. Gleisi also noted that the SRI will be temporarily led by its executive secretary until a permanent appointment is made.

item.title: Articulação de Lula contra Toffoli incomodou STF
item.description: President Lula's effort to have Supreme Court Justice Dias Toffoli recuse himself from the Master Case, following revelations of his ties to banker Daniel Vorcaro of Banco Master, was seen as judicial interference and rejected by the Court. Toffoli stepped down as rapporteur, and Lula reportedly sent emissaries to persuade him to temporarily withdraw, but the STF emphasized its internal autonomy and unity, rejecting external pressure.

item.title: Após derrota no PSD, Eduardo Leite curte comentários com críticas a Caiado
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite expressed dissatisfaction with the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate, stating the decision 'disenchants' him and perpetuates radicalized polarization in Brazil. He liked critical social media comments targeting Caiado, such as 'This Caiado won't amount to anything' and 'Good grief, Caiado?', without clarifying whether he will seek re-election or another office in 2026.

item.title: Nikolas e Janja trocam farpas nas redes por causa do PL da Misoginia
item.description: Federal Deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) and First Lady Janja Lula da Silva exchanged barbs on social media over the Misogyny Bill, which criminalizes hate speech against women. Nikolas called Janja 'silly' in a nine-minute video, claiming the bill does not combat domestic violence but seeks to control speech. Janja responded by accusing Nikolas of spreading lies while women were being killed in Minas Gerais, noting the state has the second-highest femicide rate in Brazil.

item.title: Miséria eleitoral
item.description: The PSD has decided that Ronaldo Caiado will be its presidential candidate in 2026, but his nomination hinges on a rapid rise in polling numbers to remain viable. Without such growth, the decision may be reversed due to concerns over electoral competitiveness.

item.title: Ministro de Lula diz que ‘todo mundo sabe’ quando escândalos Master e INSS começaram
item.description: The head of the CGU, Vinícius Marques de Carvalho, stated that the INSS and Banco Master scandals did not increase corruption under Lula’s government but revealed a commitment to fighting corruption that was absent before. He said 'everyone knows' these cases began during Bolsonaro’s administration, arguing that current investigations reflect the government’s anti-corruption efforts, not a rise in corruption. The remarks were made at the 10th Seminar Caminhos Contra a Corrupção at USP, amid Brazil’s worst-ever ranking in Transparency International’s Corruption Perceptions Index.

item.title: Novo Paraná Pesquisas indica uma tendência positiva para Flávio e um alerta para Lula
item.description: The Paraná Pesquisas survey shows Flávio Bolsonaro technically tied with Lula in the first round (37.8% to 41.3%) and leading in the second round (45.2% to 44.1%), signaling rapid growth for the opposition candidate. His rise is attributed to the electoral power of the Bolsonaro surname, described as unprecedented in vote transfer, and a generational inversion in the electorate, with younger voters shifting right and older voters leaning left. Polarization is intensifying, shrinking space for third-way candidates and pointing to an extremely tight runoff.

item.title: Pesquisa inédita mostra que partidos nunca falaram tanto em 'democracia'
item.description: The term 'democracy' reached a record number of mentions in speeches at the Brazilian Chamber of Deputies in 2025, with 1,888 occurrences, up from 1,480 in 2023 and down to 1,095 in 2024. In 2026, with partial data, the topic appears in only 145 debates, suggesting a possible decline or shift in focus. The unprecedented study by researcher Fábio Vasconcellos (ReDem/UFPR) highlights the intensive and contested use of the concept in recent Brazilian politics.

item.title: Caiado é mais ‘agressivo’, mas deve ficar na ‘periferia da eleição’, diz Gleisi
item.description: Institutional Relations Minister Gleisi Hoffmann said that Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), chosen by his party to run for president in 2026, is a more aggressive figure than other PSD names, but will remain on the periphery of the election due to entrenched polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. She stated there is no room for a third-way candidate in the current political landscape, which is defined by a contest between the PT leader and the Bolsonaro-aligned senator.

item.title: AtlasIntel atesta liderança de Tarcísio em SP
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on Monday, the 30th, shows Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans) leading in all four second-round scenarios tested for the São Paulo gubernatorial race. Against Fernando Haddad (PT), Tarcísio holds 53.5% of voting intentions, while Haddad trails significantly. The result confirms the incumbent governor’s advantage in his re-election bid.

item.title: Eleição será polarizada e Caiado deve ficar à margem na disputa pela Presidência, diz Gleisi
item.description: Minister Gleisi Hoffmann stated that the 2026 presidential elections will be polarized and that Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) should remain on the periphery of the main contest. She suggested Caiado will not play a central role in the race for the Presidency.

item.title: Presidente do PT de SP defende Kassab na vice de Lula e Alckmin para Senado
item.description: The president of the Workers' Party in São Paulo, federal deputy Kiko Celeguim, advocates for Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD and former mayor, to replace Geraldo Alckmin as Lula's running mate in the 2026 presidential ticket. Alckmin would then run for the Senate. The proposal signals a potential political realignment among the PT, PSD, and PSB to strengthen the governing coalition.

item.title: Aliados de Bolsonaro reclamam de regras de Moraes para domiciliar, e defesa estuda recorrer
item.description: Allies of Jair Bolsonaro complain about the decision by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes, who granted house arrest with rules deemed overly restrictive. While the move to house arrest was seen as relief by family and supporters, the defense is studying an appeal of the imposed conditions. The case highlights tensions between the judiciary and Bolsonaro’s supporters ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Atlas/Estadão: Tarcísio é melhor que Haddad para lidar com criminalidade, educação e infraestrutura
item.description: Atlas/Estadão poll shows Tarcísio de Freitas leading Fernando Haddad in the São Paulo gubernatorial race, with 49.1% first-round vote intention and a over 10-point lead in the simulated runoff. Voters view Tarcísio as more trustworthy than Haddad across all policy areas, especially infrastructure (59% vs 38%), crime (57% vs 39%), and education (55% vs 42%). Crime is cited as the state’s top problem by 60% of respondents.

item.title: Eduardo Leite reage ao anúncio da pré-candidatura de Ronaldo Caiado
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul, indirectly criticized the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its 2026 presidential pre-candidate, stating the decision deepens radicalized political polarization in Brazil, though he did not announce leaving the party. He emphasized that the pursuit of balance and common sense in politics will continue in society and reaffirmed his commitment to public service.

item.title: Quaest: Flávio Bolsonaro vence Lula entre eleitores do Espírito Santo
item.description: A Quaest poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) leading Lula (PT) in the presidential race in Espírito Santo, with 37% to 26%. Other candidates remain below 4%, while 16% are undecided and 14% would vote blank/null or not vote. The survey also found that 49% of Espírito Santo residents view the federal government negatively.

item.title: Novo vende camiseta ‘Zema presidente’ antes do início da campanha eleitoral
item.description: The Novo party sold a t-shirt with the slogan 'Brazil first: Zema president' before the official campaign period began, later removing it from the catalog after backlash. Legal experts state that explicitly naming Zema as president constitutes an illegal implicit vote request outside the allowed pre-campaign period. The party defended the sale as permitted for financial maintenance, while the online store continues selling items with only the party's symbol.

item.title: STF define semana que vem se eleição no Rio será direta ou indireta; entenda como fica o governo
item.description: Minister Edson Fachin has scheduled an in-person STF hearing for April 8 to decide whether Rio's interim gubernatorial election will be indirect (via the state legislature) or direct (by popular vote). The ruling aims to uphold constitutional legality, legal security, and institutional stability, with the winner serving until January 1, 2027. The PSD, led by Eduardo Paes, filed the action to prevent Douglas Ruas from being elected indirectly by the ALERJ, which would benefit his PL rival.

item.title: 'Tende a manter polarização', diz Eduardo Leite sobre escolha de Caiado como nome ao Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite criticized the PSD's decision to select Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, stating the choice "disenchants". Leite, also a contender for the party's nomination, argued the decision maintains political polarization.

item.title: Com foco em popularidade de Lula, debate sobre fim da "taxa das blusinhas" esquenta no Planalto
item.description: The Lula government is debating the revocation of the 'blusinhas tax,' a 20% levy on imports under $50, aiming to boost the president's popularity amid stagnant polling and rising competitiveness of Flávio Bolsonaro's candidacy. Led by political aides including Sidônio Palmeira (Secom) and Rui Costa (Casa Civil), the move faces opposition from domestic retailers and has not yet involved the Finance Ministry, though speculation exists about its facilitation under Dario Durigan. The discussion reflects cost-of-living pressures and an effort to improve public income perception, despite real wage gains above inflation being undermined by rising debt.

item.title: Kassab sobre candidatos do PSD nos Estados: vai ter quem apoie Lula, vai ter quem apoie Caiado
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab said he expects party figures to support President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) in the 2026 elections, rather than backing the party's own presidential candidate, Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado. The statement suggests a potential internal split within the PSD over presidential support in the upcoming elections.

item.title: Kassab: equilíbrio fiscal foi comprometido com Lula; Bolsonaro teve ações lamentáveis na saúde
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, criticized Lula's government for compromising fiscal balance and Bolsonaro's administration for lamentable actions in health, while commenting on Ronaldo Caiado's presidential candidacy for the PSD in 2026.

item.title: Ratinho Jr. celebra escolha de Caiado como candidato à Presidência pelo PSD
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior celebrated on social media the selection of Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as the PSD's presidential candidate, after withdrawing from the race to remain in office. He praised Caiado's nationally recognized work in education and security, and Eduardo Leite's fiscal recovery of Rio Grande do Sul, framing Caiado as a right-wing alternative to oppose Lula's government.

item.title: A resposta do governador do Maranhão ao seu pedido de afastamento no STF
item.description: Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão responded to a PCdoB petition before the STF seeking his removal over alleged nepotism and non-compliance with rulings by Minister Alexandre de Moraes. Brandão argued the move is politically motivated, stemming from the party’s break with his administration, and criticized the judicialization of partisan disputes. The background includes his rift with former governor and now STF Justice Flávio Dino. The PGR will weigh in before the relator’s decision.

item.title: Brasil vai ser melhor se Caiado for presidente, afirma Kassab
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, announced Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) as the party's presidential candidate for 2026. He stated that Caiado has the courage, energy, and experience to take on commitments, adding that 'Brazil will be better if Caiado is president'.

item.title: Ratinho Jr. elogia escolha de Caiado para disputar Presidência pelo PSD: ‘Fonte de inspiração’
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior praised the PSD's decision to nominate Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, citing Caiado's national reputation in education and security as an inspiration. He also acknowledged Eduardo Leite’s public service, though Leite later expressed disappointment with the party’s decision and criticized traditional Brazilian politics.

item.title: Bolsonaro tem mais restrições que Collor em prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted humanitarian house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro with stricter measures than those applied to Fernando Collor in 2025, including bans on cell phones, social media, and direct or indirect communication, plus limits on visits and gatherings. Bolsonaro's measure has an initial 90-day term with review, while Collor's is unlimited and based solely on age and health without communication restrictions.

item.title: Tarcísio venceria aliados de Lula em SP, diz AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel/Estado de S.Paulo poll shows that Governor Tarcísio de Freitas would win all four second-round scenarios tested against Lula-allied candidates, with leads ranging from 8.3 to 18.3 percentage points. His largest advantage is over Fernando Haddad (PT), at 53.5% to 43.2%. The survey interviewed 2,200 people in São Paulo from March 24–29, 2026, with a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: BTG/Nexus: Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro empatam em 1º e 2º turnos
item.description: Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) are tied in both first and second round scenarios for the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, according to the first wave of the BTG/Nexus poll. The technical tie reflects enduring polarization in the Brazilian electorate, with no clear advantage for either leading candidate tested. The poll was released on March 30, 2026, at 12:12 PM.

item.title: Kassab diz que PSD escolheu Caiado por ter mais chances de ir ao 2º turno
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, stated that the party chose Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate due to his higher likelihood of reaching the runoff compared to Eduardo Leite. Nevertheless, both candidates trail behind Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in voter intention polls. Leite publicly opposed the decision, arguing it reinforces a radically polarized political environment.

item.title: ACM Neto fecha chapa com PL e deve apoiar Flávio Bolsonaro na Bahia
item.description: ACM Neto has closed a ticket with the PL to run for governor of Bahia, featuring João Roma and Ângelo Coronel as Senate candidates and Zé Cocá as vice-governor. The ticket will openly support Flávio Bolsonaro for president, mirroring national polarization between PT and bolsonarism in the state. Coronel’s departure from the PSD after backing Lula weakens Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential prospects in Bahia.

item.title: Palanques estaduais expõem forças e limites de Lula e Flávio na disputa de 2026
item.description: Lula maintains consolidated support from Northeastern governors but faces declining popularity in the region, while Flávio Bolsonaro intensifies his pre-campaign in the Northeast and strengthens alliances in the South and Center-West to expand his state-level base. The battle for governors' endorsements is becoming decisive in a still fragmented landscape, seven months ahead of the 2026 presidential election.

item.title: Kassab aposta em Caiado por perfil mais combativo e governo já prepara estratégia contra candidatura
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, is backing Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, for his more combative profile to capture votes from center and non-aligned right-wing voters currently supporting Flávio Bolsonaro. Caiado’s strategy will focus on three pillars: hardline public security, fiscally responsible liberal economics, and social policies strengthened by his medical background. The Lula government is preparing a response, maintaining its strategy against Bolsonaro but adjusting rhetoric to link Caiado to hardline bolsonarism and conservative allies, with discussions underway about creating a dedicated public security ministry.

item.title: Qual é a data das eleições de 2026 para presidente, governador, senador e deputado?
item.description: The 2026 Brazilian general elections will be held in two rounds: the first round on October 4, 2026, and the second round, if needed, on October 25, 2026. Voters will elect the president, governors, senators, federal, state, and district deputies. The text also covers electoral rules such as the deadline to regularize voter registration (by May 6, 2026), the start of election campaigning (August 16, 2026), and upcoming changes to inauguration dates beginning in 2027.

item.title: Dívida continua “sustentável” em todos os cenários, diz Tesouro
item.description: National Treasury Secretary Daniel Leal stated that Brazil’s public debt remains sustainable across all projected scenarios, with stabilization expected around 84% of GDP in the coming years. Despite a primary deficit of R$30.1 billion in February and record interest payments of R$1.03 trillion over 12 months, the government emphasizes fiscal transparency and attributes rising debt service costs to the Central Bank’s contractionary monetary policy, while affirming debt sustainability is not compromised.

item.title: Preterido no PSD, Leite perde disputa interna por candidatura pela 2ª vez
item.description: Eduardo Leite suffered his second consecutive defeat in the PSD's internal presidential candidacy dispute, following Ronaldo Caiado's selection as the party's representative. Leite criticized the decision for perpetuating radical polarization and reaffirmed his commitment to a genuine liberal democratic center, without clarifying whether he will remain in office or run for another position in 2026. In 2022, he had similarly sought to launch a presidential bid via the PSDB but lost the party's primaries to João Doria and Arthur Virgílio.

item.title: Diretor-geral da PF diz que corporação ‘não protege nem persegue’: ‘A gente trabalha com isenção’
item.description: Federal Police Director-General Andrei Rodrigues stated that the agency neither protects nor persecutes anyone and operates with complete impartiality, denying any political influence in its investigations. He highlighted progress in the Banco Master case and praised the work of Central Bank President Gabriel Galípolo and Coaf head Ricardo Saadi, despite recent criticism of the agency and restrictions imposed by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on the sharing of financial intelligence reports.
